content:
💡 super | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a secure, user-friendly, and highly configurable WiFi router solution for home networks, enabling advanced network segmentation and security controls.
✨ Key Features: • One Password Per WiFi Device • Policy Based / Zero Trust Network Access • Per-Device DNS Rules & Ad Block Lists • Multi-PSK with WPA3 • Wireguard VPN
📖 Summary: SPR is an open-source, user-friendly, and secure WiFi router solution designed for home networks. It offers advanced features like per-device WiFi passwords, policy-based network access, ad blocking, and granular DNS control, enhancing both security and privacy.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 codeburn | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Observing and optimizing AI coding token expenditure by providing a detailed, local breakdown of usage across different AI tools and projects.
✨ Key Features: • Tracks token usage, cost, and performance across 20+ AI coding tools • Breaks down spending by task type, model, tool, project, and provider • Runs locally, reading session data directly from disk • Prices calls using LiteLLM
📖 Summary: CodeBurn is a local TUI dashboard for tracking AI coding token usage and costs across various AI tools. It provides detailed breakdowns of spending by task, model, project, and provider, helping developers understand and optimize their AI expenses without sending any data externally.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ open-source-games | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Discovering and accessing open-source video games and their development resources.
✨ Key Features: • Curated list of open-source games • Categorization by genre • Links to game websites • Links to source code repositories • Includes open-source remakes of commercial games
📖 Summary: This repository is a curated list of open-source video games and open-source remakes of commercial games. It categorizes games by genre, providing links to their websites and source code repositories.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 activepieces | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: AI-powered workflow automation and integration, serving as an open-source, self-hostable alternative to platforms like Zapier.
✨ Key Features: • Extensible through a type-safe pieces framework in TypeScript • Open ecosystem with community-contributed pieces available on npmjs.com
📖 Summary: Activepieces is an open-source, self-hosted workflow automation platform designed to be extensible through a type-safe TypeScript framework. It allows users to build AI-powered workflows and integrations, acting as an alternative to services like Zapier, with a strong focus on community contributions and an AI-first approach.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 ntfy | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Sending instant, scriptable push notifications to personal devices without requiring complex setup or third-party accounts.
✨ Key Features: • HTTP-based pub-sub notification service • Send notifications to phone/desktop via scripts • No sign-up or fees required for basic usage • Self-hostable open-source service • Open-source Android and iOS apps available
📖 Summary: ntfy is an open-source, HTTP-based pub-sub notification service that allows users to send push notifications to their phones or desktops from any script or computer without sign-up or fees. It provides a free hosted service at ntfy.sh and also offers open-source Android and iOS apps.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
